alt: Great blue heron in flight against a clear blue sky
remark: In flight they look almost prehistoric — all wing, barely any bird.

alt: Caspian tern in flight against a clear blue sky
remark: The red bill is what gives it away. Without it, easy to mistake for a gull at a distance.

alt: Mallard drake swimming in dark water
behavior: Drifting across still water at dusk
remark: The head only reads iridescent green in direct light. Otherwise it's just dark.

alt: Mallard hen leading a line of ducklings across dark water
behavior: Leading nine ducklings across the pond, single file
remark: None of them more than a few days old yet.

alt: Virginia rail foraging beside a path, prey in its bill
behavior: Foraging at the path's edge, out of the reeds for once
remark: Rails stay hidden. This one stepped into the open for about four seconds.
